subject: DevOps
title: Amazon DynamoDB Cascade (2015)
description: A fast breakdown of Amazon DynamoDB Cascade (2015) for builders.

hook.text: September 2015. DynamoDB slowed in us-east-1. Services retried. The retries made it worse.

buildup.text: A metadata partition overloaded. Tables became briefly unavailable. Clients started retrying.

discovery.text: Retries created more load. More load caused more failures. A feedback loop amplified the problem.

twist.text: Services without timeouts waited forever, consuming all threads. They died silently.

climax.text: AWS redesigned the metadata system. Customers learned: add timeouts and circuit breakers.

punchline.text: Retries without backoff amplify failures. Add jitter.

quiz.question: What amplified the DynamoDB outage into a cascade?
options: Aggressive client retries created a feedback loop
A physical hardware failure destroyed the servers
An attacker overwhelmed the service with traffic
